title: Universal Display reports lower results for Q1 2026
content: Universal Display Corporation reported its financial results for Q1 2026, with revenues of $142 million, 14% lower than Q1 2025 - and also lower than analyst anticipation. Net income was $36 million, down from $64 million - and UDC generated $109 in operating cash flow.

title: Advantest beats on AI chip testing, cautious outlook dents shares
content: Japan's Advantest closed its fiscal year with strong results, highlighting how AI-driven chip demand is reshaping semiconductor testing economics, even as a cautious outlook weighed on sentiment.

title: Samsung Electro-Mechanics weighs MLCC price hike of 5–10% as supply tightens
content: Samsung Electro-Mechanics is considering raising prices for multilayer ceramic capacitors (MLCCs) by about 5% to 10% as demand from artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure tightens supply, according to Korean media reports and industry sources.

title: Macronix eyes steady growth from 1Q26 amid eMMC supply gap
content: Memory maker Macronix (MXIC) is emerging from an operational slump in the first quarter of 2026, driven by explosive revenue growth in embedded multi-media cards (eMMCs) as major global players exit the multi-level cell (MLC) NAND segment. The company reported a quarterly increase of 94% and an annual surge of 3,993% in eMMC sales.

title: Samsung strike threat highlights rising labor risk to AI chip supply chain and corporate pay models
content: Samsung Electronics is facing its largest labor escalation in years after unions representing tens of thousands of workers voted to authorize strike action. The dispute centers on compensation structures, particularly performance-based bonuses linked to semiconductor profits, which workers argue have become increasingly opaque and insufficient relative to the company's record earnings in the AI-driven chip cycle.

title: KLA 3Q26: AI chip demand drives process control, guidance fails to clear bar
content: KLA Corporation's fiscal 3Q26 results underscore a familiar pattern in the current semiconductor cycle: strong execution tied to AI infrastructure demand, but investor expectations are rising even faster.

title: SanDisk 3Q26: AI demand lifts NAND, long-term deals reshape profit model
content: SanDisk's fiscal 3Q26 results point to a decisive shift in the NAND industry, with AI-driven data center demand lifting both performance and pricing, while new long-term supply agreements begin to reshape the sector's historically cyclical model.

title: Apple earnings call signals a supply-constrained handoff to the Ternus era
content: Apple's fiscal second-quarter earnings call was less about the beat and more about positioning the next phase of leadership. The message was controlled and deliberate: demand is strong, constraints are external, and incoming CEO John Ternus represents continuity with sharper hardware and AI execution.

title: Apple's fiscal 2Q in numbers: iPhone surge, China rebound, margins hold
content: Apple Inc. beat Wall Street expectations in its fiscal second quarter, with strong iPhone demand, resilient Services growth and a rebound in China driving a broad-based earnings outperformance.

title: Chip Industry Week in Review
content:
Google's TPUs now for sale; 2/3nm capacity crunch; IC tool sales to China stopped; memory shortage widens in 2027; mega-earnings; Arm's agentic toolkit; Intel seeds universities; KAIST's liquid cooling for advanced packages; MIT-IBM tool for estimating power; V2X collaboration.

The post Chip Industry Week in Review appeared first on Semiconductor Engineering.

title: Yageo AI revenue reaches 15% as chair says sector remains early in cycle
content: Yageo chairman Pierre Chen said the company has undergone a major transformation over the past eight years, driven in part by the rise of AI applications.

title: Samsung Electro-Mechanics tops US$2 billion quarterly sales, flags ABF substrate shortage
content: Samsung Electro-Mechanics (Semco) reported its first-ever quarterly revenue exceeding KRW3 trillion (approx. US$2.2 billion), driven by expanding AI infrastructure investment and rising demand for automotive electronics, marking a new scale milestone for the company.

title: Lite-On profit rises on surge in AI-driven cloud demand
content: Lite-On Technology reported first-quarter revenue of NT$43.4 billion (approx. US$1.35 billion), up 19% from a year earlier, as surging demand for AI infrastructure fueled rapid growth in its cloud-related business.

title: Foxconn Industrial Internet posts 102% net profit growth in 1Q26 on AI GPU, ASIC demand
content: Benefiting from the continued expansion of artificial intelligence (AI) computing power demand, Foxconn Industrial Internet (FII), a subsidiary of Hon Hai Precision Industry (Foxconn), reported first-quarter 2026 revenue that far exceeded market expectations. Revenue reached CNY251.08 billion (approx. US$36.74 billion), up 56.52% year over year, while net profit attributable to shareholders of the parent company reached CNY10.60 billion, soaring 102.55%, with profit growth significantly outpacin

title: MediaTek lifts 2026 ASIC target to $2 billion despite smartphone market deterioration
content: MediaTek held its earnings call on April 30, expressing a cautious outlook for the 2026 smartphone market. Despite this, growth in other applications is expected to effectively offset the decline.
